title: Foxbook typed-reference v1.0
description: Typed reference to a Foxbook transparency-log leaf, embeddable inside an evidence-layer envelope (e.g. Concordia v0.5+'s references[] array). Ratified at ADR 0009 via Discussion #73 (https://github.com/cloakmaster/foxbook/discussions/73). The typed reference is container-agnostic: how it embeds inside a specific envelope shape is the evidence-layer's spec to define; this schema specifies only the typed reference's content.

typed_reference_version.description: Schema version. v1.0 is the ratified form; future additive evolution stays within v1.x per ADR 0004 precedent. Breaking changes require v2.0.

tl_url.description: Base URL of the transparency log carrying the leaf. Verifiers resolve ${tl_url}/inclusion/${leaf_index} and ${tl_url}/leaf/${leaf_index} against this base.

leaf_index.description: Position of the leaf in the log. Combined with tl_url, uniquely identifies the leaf.

tl_leaf_canonical_hash.description: RFC 9162 leaf hash of the leaf at leaf_index. Lets verifiers detect log forks by recomputing from the live leaf and comparing. A mismatch is a log-integrity violation or a falsified typed reference.

verified_signing_key_hex.description: A fact about Foxbook's log state at the moment foxbookVerify returned — specifically, the active signing key registered for this DID. NOT a claim that any specific AgentCard JWS was verified against this key. AgentCard signature verification is the verdict layer's responsibility; the typed reference provides the key bytes the verdict layer needs to do that verification, nothing more.

sth_at_verify_time.description: Optional in v1.0; promoted to required in v2.0. The Signed Tree Head observed at verify-time. Anchors temporal context + enables Merkle-root reconstruction without re-fetching the live /root. Verdict layers wanting offline-verifiable receipts SHOULD prefer typed references with this populated.

sth_signature.description: Optional in v1.0; promoted to required in v2.0. EdDSA signature over the STH content as a compact-JWS. Lets verdict layers authenticate the STH offline without trusting the integrator. Cross-checks against sth_at_verify_time catch typed-reference falsification at two independent points.
